override.reason: All current validators are green (`vp run check -r`, `vp run test:e2e:website`, `go test ./apps/backend/internal/... -count=1 -parallel 4`, and bootstrap tests). The remaining blocking review findings are stale historical reviews superseded by later completed fix features: runtime alignment by `foundation-e2e-runtime-and-format-fix`, shared-schema implementation by `foundation-pgtest-shared-schema-implementation`, CSV import bootstrap fix by `foundation-bootstrap-csv-import-email-fix`, and concrete async-sensitive guardrail usage by `foundation-async-real-path-usage-fix`.

blockingIssues.description: The reviewed feature fixed comma-separated PATCH time_entry_ids binding but did not deliver the promised 5173/8080 local runtime/readiness alignment work, and the session evidence still showed frontend 5173 unreachable.

The reviewed feature normalized ephemeral opentoggl_test_* schemas instead of implementing the required shared opentoggl_test topology, omitted concrete async guardrails, and lacked the required browser smoke proof.

The reviewed feature left TestImportRoutesAcceptTimeEntriesCSVUpload unresolved at the time and only demonstrated async queue/drain helpers in synthetic platform tests rather than wiring them into a real async-sensitive path.

The reviewed feature added browser smoke proof under a non-canonical .real-runtime.spec.ts filename and kept async coverage at helper-only synthetic tests instead of a real async-sensitive backend path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers not to claim topology/runtime fixes as complete unless the implementation and verification evidence in the same feature cover the full contract, especially when shared-state files are being updated.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: foundation-runtime-schema-alignment and foundation-shared-schema-and-async-guardrails-fix both showed mismatch between the reviewed feature scope/evidence and what shared-state guidance claimed was already true.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that browser-lane features must finish in the canonical E2E state: new Playwright E2E files should use standard *.spec.ts naming and should reuse existing auth/runtime helpers where available.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: foundation-browser-lane-and-async-usage-fix introduced foundation-smoke.real-runtime.spec.ts and reimplemented auth/bootstrap inline, both of which were corrected later.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that the reused website runtime on port 5173 must bind to 127.0.0.1 for the browser lane fix on this checkout, alongside the existing 5173/8080 readiness rules.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: foundation-e2e-runtime-and-format-fix now relies on `.factory/services.yaml` using `vp run website#dev -- --port 5173 --host 127.0.0.1`, but the library guidance still only records the ports and readiness probes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce repository exploration expectations and repo-local shared-state paths so workers avoid wasting steps on shell find/cat usage or the wrong absolute services path.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: foundation-bootstrap-csv-import-email-fix first probed /Users/opentoggl-ci/.factory/services.yaml, and foundation-importing-export-isolation-fix used shell find/cat even though repo guidance prefers Read/Grep/Glob and the services file lives under the repo root.

rejectedObservations.observation: Update shared-state guidance to say async helper coverage is already fully implemented everywhere.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because reviews still show the concrete async-sensitive-path requirement was not satisfied by the originally reviewed features.

rejectedObservations.observation: Apply an immediate services/library update based on the failed foundation-test-topology-and-browser-lane cleanup command review.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because the current repository already contains the corrected query-driven cleanup command, so no additional update is needed in this scrutiny round.
